Assonia pseudopopulus is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the family Malpighiaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and fever, due to its reported antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Assonia pseudopopulus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Assonia pseudopopulus is commonly used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often combined with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
